title: kb.edge.invalid v1
description: Emitted by kb from two distinct call sites with two distinct payload shapes, unified under one channel: (a) src/commands/codemap.rs:1600 — codemap seam-groundedness check, uses seam_id/literal/endpoints/reason/asserted_by; (b) src/commands/edges.rs:481 — related-edge validation (dangling/archived/contradiction findings), uses source_id/target_id/rel instead. Only `project` and `kind` are common to both; schema is intentionally permissive (additionalProperties: true) rather than a strict oneOf. Source: /kb.

project.description: Project the invalid edge was found in.

kind.description: codemap.rs call site: SeamKind::as_str() value (e.g. 'http-route', 'bus-channel', 'socket-port', 'process-spawn', 'env-var', 'cli-invocation'). edges.rs call site: FindingKind::as_str() value ('DANGLING', 'ARCHIVED_TARGET', 'NON_RECIPROCAL_CONTRADICTION', 'MISLABELED_ALIGNMENT', 'UNGROUNDED_CONTRADICTION'). Left as free-form string since the two call sites draw from different vocabularies under the same key.

seam_id.description: codemap.rs call site only: the seam whose source is gone.

literal.description: codemap.rs call site only: the literal string the seam was parsed from.

endpoints.description: codemap.rs call site only: number of endpoints the seam had.

reason.description: codemap.rs call site only: currently always the literal 'seam-source-gone'.

asserted_by.description: codemap.rs call site only: currently always the literal 'codemap'.

source_id.description: edges.rs call site only: source entry id of the invalid relation edge.

target_id.description: edges.rs call site only: target entry id of the invalid relation edge.

rel.description: edges.rs call site only: relation type (RelationType::as_str(), src/kb_ops/types.rs).
